description_long: This is a tall, slender ceramic vase with a narrow neck, a flared mouth, and a single curved handle attached from the neck to the shoulder. The body is decorated with intricate black-figure artwork reminiscent of ancient Greek pottery, featuring human figures engaged in dynamic scenes, possibly mythological or ceremonial. The main color scheme is a natural beige clay background with black painted details, bordered by a repeating Greek key meander pattern near the top and bottom. The handle and neck are solid black, contrasting with the lighter body. The base is slightly flared and also features black detailing. The surface appears smooth and well-preserved, with no visible chips or cracks.
description: A tall, slender Greek-style ceramic vase with black-figure artwork, a single handle, and a flared mouth, featuring intricate mythological scenes and geometric borders.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main black-figure scene with two human figures in dynamic poses, bordered by a Greek key pattern and the handle visible on the left.
description_view_1: This view highlights the vase's slender profile and the continuation of the geometric border, with the artwork partially visible and the handle mostly hidden.
description_view_2: The back view reveals a different black-figure scene with two figures flanking a central motif, and the handle is now on the right side.
description_view_3: This side view emphasizes the vase's elegant shape, with the artwork wrapping around and the handle not visible.
attribute_pattern: Greek key meander and black-figure scenes
